questionTextSource: You want to hitch a trailer with a maximum authorised mass over 750 kg to your car. What must you ensure in this situation?

Check this with hand and eye. In addition, trailers with a permissible total mass of over 750 kg must also be secured with a tear -off rope. Check before driving as to whether it is appropriate.
After you have checked all the connections, you must also check the lighting before starting drive.

questionTextSource: You are driving a car with a fully loaded trailer equipped with overrun braking. What special aspect should be noted here?

So that the trailer does not roll away when you separate it from the towing vehicle, you should put on the pendant's parking brake. The trailer brake can overheat during longer downhill rides. In this case, you may have to take breaks when you leave.
The brake brake is only activated when the trailer on the towing vehicle. If you drive uphill, this may not be the case when braking, depending on how strong you brake.

questionTextSource: What can affect the driving stability of a towed trailer?
explanationSource:
The load of a trailer should be distributed so that the center of gravity is as close as possible to the axle of the trailer. This ensures optimal driving stability and controllability of the trailer.
A load center of gravity that is far back can have a serious impact on the driving stability of your trailer. This can cause the trailer to sway more easily during sudden steering movements or at higher speeds, increasing the risk of an accident.
If the tire pressure on your trailer is too low, it can affect the driving stability of your trailer. On the one hand, low tire pressure leads to poorer road grip, which makes your trailer more uncontrollable when cornering. On the other hand, low tire pressure leads to increased tire wear, which increases the risk of tire damage while driving.
A shock absorber that is already leaking can also have a negative impact on the driving stability of your trailer. The leaking shock absorber on your trailer loses its damping properties, which can lead to unstable driving behavior and dangerous situations that are difficult to control.
answerASource: A centre of gravity that is far back in the trailer
answerBSource: Insufficient tyre pressure in the trailer tyres

questionTextSource: A single-axle trailer has an actual gross weight of 600 kg. What is the minimum possible vertical load?
explanationSource: The bearing load is the mass that rests on the coupling device of the towing vehicle. To determine it, the car driver can place a piece of wood or a pipe underneath the coupling point of the trailer and place a bathroom scale underneath. The bearing load is displayed. The minimum load to be carried by the towing vehicle shall not be less than 4% of the trailer's total weight for trailers with a gross vehicle weight of not more than 3.5 tons. However, a minimum load of 25 kg is sufficient. In this example, the minimum support load is 24 kg. When loading the trailer, you, as a car driver, must be careful not to undercut or exceed the minimum and maximum permitted load. By shifting the load, you can influence the bearing load.

questionTextSource: You are driving a truck with a maximum authorised mass of more than 3.5 t for commercial transport purposes. What period of time is deemed to constitute a driving break for you?
explanationSource:
A break from driving is time that the driver spends recovering. During this time, the driver is not behind the steering wheel of the vehicle and does not carry out any driving activities. Work such as loading and unloading the vehicle is also forbidden during this time. 
Time spent by the driver in the passenger seat is also a break from driving.

answerCSource: - spent loading and unloading the vehicle

This compensation serves to compensate for the recovery deficits caused by the shortening and to ensure traffic safety.

explanationSource: The total mass in a train (two-axle truck, three-axle trailer, no combined transport) must not exceed 40 weigh t.

questionTextSource: How can you check by calculation whether the combination of an articulated motor vehicle (semitrailer load = vertical load) exceeds the permissible total mass of the combination of vehicles?
explanationSource: You can mathematically that the maximum mass of the tractor plus determine the maximum authorized mass of the trailer and the result expected minus the Aufliegelast. So you can check whether the maximum permissible laden mass of the train is exceeded.
answerASource: Permissible total mass of the truck tractor plus permissible total mass of the semitrailer minus vertical load
answerBSource: Permissible total mass of the truck tractor plus permissible total mass of the semitrailer
answerCSource: Permissible total mass of the truck tractor plus useful load of the semitrailer

questionTextSource:
You are combining a semitrailer motor vehicle with 5 axles (not combined traffic). The following vehicle data are known to you:
Permissible total mass (Truck tractor) = 25 t
Permissible total mass (Semitrailer) = 34 t
Semitrailer load/Vertical load (Truck tractor) = 16 t
Semitrailer load/Vertical load (Semitrailer) = 16 t
explanationSource: The operation of this combination is permitted if you t at least three renounce payload, because the total load is 43 tonnes. (34 + t 25 t - 16 t = 43 t).
answerASource: - is allowed if you carry at least 3 t less than the useful load
answerBSource: - is allowed also if the semitrailer is fully loaded with 34 t

questionTextSource: How do you determine the maximum authorised combination mass of a vehicle combination consisting of a towing vehicle with rigid drawbar trailer?
explanationSource:
You calculate the total permissible towed mass using the following data:
Maximum authorised mass of the towing vehicle plus the maximum authorised mass of the trailer minus the drawbar load of the trailer, if this is greater than the maximum authorised drawbar load of the towing vehicle.
answerASource: - maximum authorised mass of the trailer less the vertical load of the trailer, if this is greater than the permissible vertical load of the towing vehicle
answerBSource: - maximum authorised mass of the trailer less the vertical load of the trailer, if this is less than the permissible vertical load of the towing vehicle
answerCSource: - load capacity of the trailer less the vertical load of the trailer, if this is greater than the permissible vertical load of the towing vehicle

questionTextSource: What is the maximum permissible dimension X for truck tractors?
explanationSource: Coming soon...

questionTextSource: What is the maximum permissible dimension X for agricultural truck tractors?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerBSource: 8 m

questionTextSource: The Road Haulage Act applies to commercial haulage vehicles or vehicle combinations in excess of what gross vehicle weight rating?
explanationSource: From a maximum mass of 3.5 t for motor vehicles or vehicle combinations with businesslike transport of goods is the road transport law observed.
answerASource: Over 3.5 t
answerBSource: 2.8 t and over

questionTextSource: The validity period of one of your driving licence classes has expired. What are the consequences of this?
explanationSource: The consequences are the driving motor vehicles in this class is prohibited and the license of this class is extinguished. It must not be used for private trips.
answerASource: The driving licence has ended for this class
answerBSource: It is prohibited to drive motor vehicles in this class
answerCSource: The driving licence for this class may only still be used for private journeys

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of when using navigation systems and road maps?
explanationSource: When using a navigation system, you should be aware that even current content can deviate from reality and content can become outdated shortly after purchase. There is no guarantee that the content is up to date despite automatic updating.
answerASource: The contents may become outdated just a short time after purchase
answerBSource: Even up-to-date contents may differ from reality
answerCSource: All navigation systems can update themselves automatically

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of when route planning abroad?
explanationSource: Before driving, find out which regulations apply abroad.
answerASource: - applicable conditions for inner city road use (a city toll for example)
answerBSource: - labelling requirements for driving in environment zones

questionTextSource: What must you take into account when planning routes?
explanationSource: For your route planning please pay attention to possible traffic delays due to road works and possible traffic delays by holiday traffic in different provinces.
answerASource: Possible traffic obstructions due to holiday traffic in different federal states
answerBSource: Possible traffic obstructions due to building sites
answerCSource: A route which is as interesting as possible

questionTextSource: Can information in road maps deviate from actual conditions?
explanationSource: Road maps may be shortly overhauled after the release and outdated. Eighth please as possible to the date.
answerASource: Road maps can be outdated even before they are published
answerBSource: Road maps are valid for 4 years and then have to be replaced
answerCSource: Road maps issued by automobile clubs and petrol companies never deviate from actual conditions

questionTextSource: What does the scale 1:300,000 on a road map tell you?
explanationSource: This standard says that 1 centimeter equals on the road map 3 kilometers.
answerASource: 1 centimetre on the map corresponds to 3 kilometres
answerBSource: 1 centimetre on the map corresponds to 300 kilometres
answerCSource: 1 centimetre on the map corresponds to 30 kilometres

questionTextSource: You are driving along the autobahn and want to go directly to Unterbach. Where do you leave the autobahn?
explanationSource: If you want to Unterbach, you should exit onto the highway exit Dusseldorf / Erkrath.
answerASource: At the Düsseldorf/Erkrath autobahn exit
answerBSource: At the Düsseldorf-Süd autobahn intersection

questionTextSource: You want to drive your vehicle from Hamburg to Rome. Which map scale should you use for initial overall planning?
explanationSource: When your trip from Hamburg to Rome you can with the scale of 1: 500,000 to carry out a first overall route planning.

questionTextSource: Which information is given by the key on a roadmap?
explanationSource: A legend of a road map provides you an explanation of the symbols and characters used in the card and a distance ruler for quick removal determination.
answerASource: Explanation of the symbols and signs on the map
answerBSource: Ruler to quickly establish distances
answerCSource: Historical overview on the history of the map

questionTextSource: Autobahn numbers have the following meanings:
explanationSource: The numbering on motorways mean: Regional highways leading three-digit numbers, and north-south highways leading odd numbers.
answerASource: North/South autobahns have uneven numbers
answerBSource: Regional autobahns have three-digit numbers

questionTextSource: You want to drive your truck with a total permissible mass of more than 7.5 t on the autobahn from Hanover to Munich. Which average speed can you reckon with?
explanationSource: With your trucks over 7.5 t permissible total mass on the highway from Hannover to Munich you should expect h with an average speed of 70 km /.
answerASource: 70 km/h
answerBSource: 80 km/h

questionTextSource: You want to transport live animals over a large distance. What do you have to consider when planning the route?
explanationSource: If you want to transport live animals over long distances, you should consider when planning the route Animal Transport Regulation, the charge control and charge maintenance and service stations and rest stops.
answerASource: Checks and maintenance of the load
answerBSource: Stops for fuel and rests

questionTextSource: You want to establish a route plan to drive from Frankfurt (Oder) to Kiel. You have to exchange loads at different intermediate stops. Which information is necessary to plan your route in terms of roads and time?
explanationSource: In your directions from Frankfurt (Oder) to Kiel, with different intermediate stations, because of charge exchange, the following information should be provided: starting point, destination, order of the waypoints, planned charging periods and breaks.
answerASource: Starting point, destination, sequence of intermediate stops, scheduled loading times and travel interruptions
answerBSource: Location, destination, intermediate stops, maximum speed

questionTextSource: You are planning your trip using a road map with a scale of 1:200,000. 1 cm on the map corresponds to how many kilometres in reality?
explanationSource: In your planning with a road map with a scale of 1: 200,000 corresponds to 1 cm 2 kilometers on the map.
answerASource: 2 km
answerBSource: 10 km

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of when using a navigation system in the vehicle?
explanationSource: When using a navigation system, please note that the information issued are recommendations, the navigation device should not interfere with your view and the navigation system should not be lying around unsecured in the car.
answerASource: The information provided consists of recommendations
answerBSource: The navigation device should not impede the driver's field of vision
answerCSource: The navigation device should not lie around in the car unattached

questionTextSource: What activities could pose a highly risky distraction while driving?
explanationSource: All three activities distract the car driver from traffic during the journey. The car driver might not be able to recognize dangerous situation in time.
answerASource: Entering an address in the sat-nav
answerBSource: Writing a text message
answerCSource: Using the telephone even through a hands-free unit
